mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 5%, restrictive discount rates dominate near-term earnings news and reduce equity duration values, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in PAYEMS signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, raising expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS trend has nonpositive Rank IC with 60-day returns over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows or expansionary payroll signals do not outperform baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: High CPIAUCSL combined with high FEDFUNDS indicates inflation pressure that keeps policy restrictive, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL above its 12-month moving average and FEDFUNDS > 5.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS signal produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered cases or adds no predictive power over FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates resilient household spending that supports near-term revenue expectations for index constituents, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS growth has Rank IC below 0.03 for 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window or positive RSAFS signals fail to beat unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below 1300 signals weakening housing demand and tighter interest-rate transmission to cyclicals, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-1300 episodes produce nonnegative average 60-day returns or the downside hit rate is below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: INDPRO above 103 reflects firm industrial output and better operating leverage for cyclical sectors, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO-above-103 signals have negative average 20-day returns or no statistically positive difference versus below-threshold observations at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: UNRATE above 3.7% signals emerging labor-market slack from a low-unemployment base, reducing expected consumption and earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-above-3.7 signals are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases or if the signal has negative downside classification skill.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDPC1 with UNRATE below 4% despite FEDFUNDS above 5% identifies a soft-landing regime in which earnings resilience outweighs discount-rate pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1, UNRATE, and FEDFUNDS condition fails to outperform unconditional 60-day returns or adds no accuracy over a FEDFUNDS-only threshold model.
